question: How many melds and pairs does a standard Taiwanese Mahjong winning hand require?
explanation: A winning hand (Hu) in Taiwanese Mahjong requires 5 melds + 1 pair = 17 tiles total (5×3 + 2). You hold 16 tiles, and the 17th — either drawn or claimed — completes the hand.

question: What meld do these three tiles form?
explanation: Three identical tiles = Pong. A Chow needs three consecutive numbers in the same suit; a Kong needs four identical tiles.

question: What meld do these three tiles form?
explanation: Three consecutive tiles of the same suit = Chow. 1-2-3 Characters is a valid sequence. Chow can only be made from numbered suits (Bamboo, Circles, Characters).

question: Can Wind tiles form a Chow?
options: Yes, East-South-West is a valid Chow
No, Winds can only form Pongs or Kongs
Yes, but only with matching seat winds
Only if all four winds are used
explanation: Wind and Dragon tiles cannot form Chow sequences — they can only be used in Pongs or Kongs.

question: You win by drawing the last tile yourself, without having claimed any discards all game. How many Tai is that?
explanation: Concealed Self-Draw is its own combined bonus worth +3 Tai — not just Self-Draw + Concealed added separately. It means: no claimed discards all game, AND you draw the winning tile yourself.

question: How many Tai does a Full Flush earn?
explanation: Full Flush (all tiles from one suit, no honor tiles) earns +8 Tai — one of the highest single bonuses in Taiwanese Mahjong.

question: You won by claiming a discard from another player. Which bonus applies?
explanation: Concealed Hand requires no claimed tiles at all. Self-Draw requires drawing the winning tile yourself from the wall. Claiming a discard to win earns neither bonus.

question: You drew your 17th tile. Which tile should you discard?
description: You have 4 complete melds and a pair. The tile you just drew connects with one tile in your hand — figure out which tile no longer belongs.
explanation: 6 Bamboo pairs with 7 Bamboo — keep them! 3 Circle has no partner and doesn't fit any meld. Discard it.
